# UC-22.2.36 · NIS2 OT Network Segmentation Validation

## Description

Aggregates firewall sessions that cross Purdue-style zone pairs your policy marks as forbidden—surfacing repeated SMB, RDP, or HTTP crossings from IT VLANs toward OT cells where the lookup says those conversations should never succeed in bulk.

## Value

Plant safety and production uptime depend on hard boundaries; this gives OT governance teams repeatable evidence that the choke-point firewalls actually see—and log—the east-west attempts that bypass paper diagrams, so regulators trust the segmentation story.

## Implementation

(1) Normalize `src_zone`/`dest_zone` naming between Palo Alto (`pan:traffic`) and Cisco ASA extractions. (2) Maintain `nis2_ot_segment_policy.csv` rows for each allowed/blocked pair with `allowed=true|false`. (3) Heavy forwarders parse locally; avoid sending raw pcaps to Splunk. (4) Save alert `UC-22.2.36` hourly; route to OT SOC distribution list. (5) Weekly workstation with plant engineering to clear false positives via exception register updates.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263) recommended for asset & identity joins; Palo Alto TA (Splunkbase 2757) and Cisco ASA TA (Splunkbase 1620) on search heads + indexers.
• Dedicated `index=ot_network` or reuse `index=firewall` with strict **RBAC** limiting analysts to OT VLAN tags.
• Field aliases in `props.conf` guaranteeing `src_zone` and `dest_zone` exist for both vendors—use `EVAL-` lines if Palo Alto uses `vsys` naming.
• Lookup `nis2_ot_segment_policy.csv` version-controlled in Git with Splunk Deployment Server push.
• Network time sync (NTP) within **1 second** on OT firewalls to align sessions.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
On Palo Alto, enable zone labels in traffic logs; forward via syslog to HF with `UDP 514` or `TLS` syslog‑ng. Map to `sourcetype=pan:traffic` per TA defaults.

For Cisco ASA in OT demilitarized zones, enable `logging threat` and zoning via context; ingest into same index with unique `fw_cluster` field.

Validate with `index=firewall sourcetype=pan:traffic | stats dc(src_zone)` expecting >3 zones.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Base search filters last **24 hours**; `lookup` attaches `allowed`. Weed out noise with `sessions>10`. Extend with `where protocol="tcp" AND dest_port IN (445,3389)` for classic lateral movement focus.

Consider correlating IDS `sourcetype=suricata` signatures using `join` on `src_ip` after `rename`.

Schedule hourly; throttle by `src_zone`,`dest_zone` pair for **12 hours**.

Step 3 — Validate
During maintenance, attempt controlled RDP from CORP-PC subnet toward HMI VLAN in lab—confirm row appears with accurate `bytes` and `sessions` vs firewall session browser.

Importantly, verify denied vs allowed semantics: some devices log even blocked attempts—set `where action="allow"` if your policy tracks only successful crossings.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard with Sankey edges weighted by `bytes`; clicking edge drills into raw events (Events panel in ES Security Intelligence).

Create adaptive response notable in ES with playbooks linking to work order system.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **Lookup misses** — default `allowed` null behaves like false positive; set `OUTPUTNEW` carefully or `fillnull allowed="true"` for unknown pairs awaiting classification. • **Huge `bytes` spikes** — backups crossing zones; cross-check `app=smb` vs NetBackup hosts; use `where NOT backup_flag=1` after tagging. • **No zones** — logging template missing; open Palo Alto UI Device → Log Settings. • **ASA field blank** — interface labels not mapped; edit `transforms.conf` zone extraction. • **Performance** — narrow `index=firewall earliest=-4h` during incident storms.


## SPL

```spl
index=netfw earliest=-24h src_zone=* dest_zone=*
| lookup nis2_ot_segment_policy.csv src_zone dest_zone OUTPUT allowed protocol port
| where allowed="false"
| stats sum(bytes) as bytes count as sessions by src_zone dest_zone dest_port app
| where sessions > 10
| sort - bytes
| table src_zone, dest_zone, dest_port, app, bytes, sessions
```

## Visualization

Sankey (zone to zone), Table (violations), Single value (distinct forbidden paths), Heatmap (sessions by hour).
